features.label: Route and registry entry
features.evidence: tools/pdf-workspace/index.html exists; registry id pdf-workspace points to /tools/pdf-workspace/; sitemap-tools.xml includes the route.
features.limitations: The route is implemented as one large inline page rather than a separated app module.

features.label: Initial PDF upload
features.evidence: #fileIn accepts .pdf/application/pdf; local browser smoke uploaded audit-results/pdf-lib-test.pdf and opened the workspace.
features.limitations: No explicit size gate or validation beyond PDF parsing errors.

features.label: Add PDF / merge pages
features.evidence: #tbAdd opens #mergeIn with multiple files; addPDFs copies all pages from added PDFs after the current page.
features.limitations: No merge review queue, no per-file order before insertion, no size guard, skipped files are only surfaced as toasts.

features.label: PDF preview rendering
features.evidence: PDF.js loads from local vendor assets and rendered a canvas in desktop and mobile smoke tests.
features.limitations: No selectable text layer, search layer, form layer, or lazy page virtualization.

features.label: Edited PDF download
features.evidence: #tbDL uses email-gate-modal before buildPDF and dlB creates a PDF Blob download.
features.limitations: Download metadata relies on gate events and local pdfSaveOp; generated PDF events are not explicitly dispatched for all exports.

features.label: Split PDF
features.evidence: Split modal opens and produces multiple PDFs inside a manual ZIP when split points are selected.
features.limitations: Only split-point flow, not arbitrary named ranges. ZIP creation is custom code and should get binary regression tests.

features.label: Rearrange pages
features.evidence: Rearrange modal opens and drag/drop updates the in-memory page order.
features.limitations: Desktop drag pattern only; limited keyboard and touch affordances.

features.label: Rotate page
features.evidence: #tbRotate rotates the selected page by 90 degrees and buildPDF applies the rotation on export.
features.limitations: Current page only, no all-pages or selected-pages command, no undo stack.

features.label: Page numbers
features.evidence: Modal supports start number, font size, and six positions, then draws page numbers with pdf-lib.
features.limitations: No page range, prefix/suffix, total-pages format, margins, font choice, or preview placement.

features.label: Compress PDF
features.evidence: Compress modal opens and re-saves through pdf-lib with object stream options.
features.limitations: This is optimization/rewrite, not true compression. It does not downsample images, remove hidden objects, subset fonts, or report before/after size before download.

features.label: Add text
features.evidence: Text modal draws new text onto the selected page with pdf-lib.
features.limitations: Overlay-only. It cannot select, edit, delete, reflow, or replace existing PDF text.

features.label: Edit existing PDF text
features.evidence: No text extraction editing model, content stream editing, OCR correction, or text object selection is implemented.
features.limitations: Current text behavior is add-only overlay drawing.

features.label: Signature
features.evidence: Signature modal provides a canvas pad and embeds the drawing as a PNG.
features.limitations: Visual signature only. No certificate signing, identity proof, saved signature library, or movable placement handles.

features.label: Image insert
features.evidence: Image modal accepts PNG/JPG/WebP, converts WebP through canvas, and embeds the image.
features.limitations: Numeric placement only. No drag placement, resize handles, layer order, crop, opacity, or image compression controls.

features.label: Highlight
features.evidence: Highlight modal draws a colored rectangle on the selected page.
features.limitations: This is a rectangle overlay, not a PDF text highlight annotation bound to selected text.

features.label: Watermark
features.evidence: Watermark modal draws text across all pages with size, opacity, color, and rotation controls.
features.limitations: Text only, all pages only, no image watermark, no tiled watermark, no page range.

features.label: Extract pages
features.evidence: Extract modal accepts comma/range syntax and downloads a new PDF containing those copied pages.
features.limitations: No preview of selected pages and no duplicate-page warning.

features.label: Stamp
features.evidence: Stamp modal offers fixed text presets, color, size, opacity, and all-page toggle.
features.limitations: Preset-only text stamp, fixed center placement and rotation, no custom stamp library.

features.label: Redact
features.evidence: Redact modal draws a black rectangle over content.
features.limitations: Not true redaction. Underlying text, images, and metadata can remain in the PDF and may be extractable.

features.label: Crop
features.evidence: Crop modal sets PDF crop boxes from numeric margins.
features.limitations: No visual crop handles, no validation for invalid crop boxes beyond thrown errors, no page preview.

features.label: Password protect
features.evidence: Protect modal calls the local QPDF WebAssembly wrapper with AES-256 flags and permissions.
features.limitations: Needs end-to-end encrypted-output verification. Mobile toolbar access to Protect is poor because it sits off-screen in horizontal scroll.

features.label: Page thumbnail system
features.evidence: PDF.js renders JPEG data URLs for every page and the sidebar uses them for select/delete/rotation display.
features.limitations: All thumbnails render eagerly and live in memory. No lazy loading, recycling, or progress cancellation for large PDFs.

features.label: Object / annotation layer
features.evidence: No persistent object store or overlay layer exists for text, images, signatures, highlights, stamps, redactions, or crops.
features.limitations: Applied objects are burned into new PDF bytes immediately and cannot be selected, moved, edited, undone, or reordered.

features.label: Toolbar command system
features.evidence: 17 top-level toolbar buttons are laid out in one horizontal row. Browser smoke measured toolbar scrollWidth 2179px at 1366px and 1920px viewports.
features.limitations: Actions overflow until very wide desktop. There is no command grouping, menu, tool mode state, keyboard command layer, or command palette.

features.label: Mobile responsive behavior
features.evidence: Mobile smoke at 390px opened the workspace, rendered one page, and showed the bottom zoom/download bar.
features.limitations: Toolbar still scrolls horizontally at 390px. Crop and Protect were off-screen enough that Playwright could not click them from the initial toolbar position.

features.label: Production debug / inspector UI
features.evidence: Source search and browser smoke found no visible debug or inspector UI. debugLikeVisible was empty at desktop and mobile.
features.limitations: There is a source comment saying Save/Share buttons need manual placement, but it is not visible UI.

features.label: Analytics events
features.evidence: Page loads GA config G-D859CGF391 and pdf-download-gate emits pdf_download_gate_passed when a gated download passes.
features.limitations: No per-command events for upload, split, rearrange, rotate, compress, text, sign, image, highlight, watermark, extract, stamp, redact, crop, or protect. Body lacks tool-page class, so core tool timing/scroll analytics are not clearly active.

features.label: Error handling
features.evidence: Most async operations catch errors and show toast messages; core bundle also provides a global error banner.
features.limitations: Errors are terse and not recoverable. Large-PDF memory failures, encrypted-PDF failures, invalid crop boxes, and QPDF initialization failures need more specific UX.

features.label: Large PDF behavior
features.evidence: The app keeps whole PDF bytes in memory, slices buffers repeatedly, renders every page and thumbnail, and reserializes full documents on each applied change.
features.limitations: No file size budget, page count budget, virtualization, cancellation, worker offload for pdf-lib operations, or low-memory mode.
